sourceMovie.title: Full Metal Jacket
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Full Metal Jacket (1987) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Stanley Kubrick's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and war blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Apocalypse Now
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Full Metal Jacket clicked for you, Apocalypse Now (1979) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and war, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Full Metal Jacket clicked for you, Platoon (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and war, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Full Metal Jacket?
faq.answer: Try Apocalypse Now, Platoon, Civil War—each shares DNA with Full Metal Jacket while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Full Metal Jacket?
faq.answer: Full Metal Jacket (1987) is best described as Drama, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Full Metal Jacket worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
